JavaScript Objects: Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? (Core Concepts 29)

Is JavaScript Call by Reference or Call by Sharing? In this post we walk through evaluation strategies — Call by value, Call by reference, and Call by sharing — with simple examples, and figure out which one JavaScript actually uses when you pass objects around.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (30) - The Game Loop

Time to build the game loop. In short, it all comes down to app.ticker.add((delta) => {...});. In this part we wire up an HP bar that ticks down over time, hook the collision detection into the loop, and black out the screen once HP hits zero.
JavaScript Core (28) - Objects - The Object Reference Exercise

This chapter is the exercise for object references. The whole point is to make sure the "objects are passed by reference" idea actually stuck. We walk through a tricky little snippet step by step, tracing memory pointers and drawing out exactly why a and b end up the way they do.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (29) - Building treasureHunter (the Treasure Hunter)

Time to actually start building treasureHunter, our little Treasure Hunter game. In this part we'll slowly pull the pieces apart and put them back together to get a simple version up and running — loading the spritesheet, cutting out each sprite, and laying out the dungeon, monster, treasure, door, and player.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (28) - Loading Images from JSON

Last chapter we learned how to crop images. This time we'll look at another way to load them — using a JSON file. With a JSON spritesheet you can slice up one image into multiple sprites, and if anything changes later you just tweak the JSON. This post walks through how it works in PixiJS V5.
JavaScript Core Concepts (27) — Objects: How Object References Actually Work

Last time we talked about pass-by-reference for objects, but the explanation was still pretty fuzzy. So this time let's actually see how it works under the hood — what happens in memory when you copy a primitive, and what happens when you assign an object to another variable and change its properties.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (27) - Cropping Images with Rectangle and Texture

Next up we're going to load images through JSON, but before that let's learn how to crop an image. In this post I'll show you how to pull a single piece out of a sprite sheet using PIXI.Rectangle, why your sprites get overwritten when you reuse the same texture, and how copying the Texture fixes it.
JavaScript Core Concepts (26): Objects — Why Changing One Object Changes Another

Time to talk about passing objects by reference. If you don't get how reference semantics work in JavaScript, it's really easy to step on a landmine. We'll walk through call by value vs call by reference, why changing one variable can secretly change another, and the simplest way to spot when reference behavior is in play.
JavaScript Core (25): Why Reading a Missing Object Property Gives You undefined (and Sometimes Crashes)

Reading a property that doesn't exist on an object hands you undefined — but go one level deeper and suddenly JavaScript throws an error instead. This post digs into why that happens, how operator precedence and associativity cause it, why primitives can't hold their own properties, and the two simple ways to avoid the crash.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (26) - Treasure Hunter: Building a Real Mini-Game

Time to put everything together. From here on we'll start combining the techniques from earlier in the series to build an actual little game called Treasure Hunter. This post covers where the game assets come from, which sprites we'll be using, and why loading images through a JSON file beats loading them one by one.
